metadata.title:
Americas Hugest Crop Waving Tassels-Farmers Own
Autos-To Feed 80 Per Cent Of It to Live Stock

BY J. v. KNIGHT

pages.raw_text:
OMAHA, Aug. 21.-The corn
crop of the United States this
year will be the largest ever har-
vested. It covers an area of no..
100000 acres, and the yield will
be 5000, 100000 bushels.


Figures mean little when con-
sidered in the light Of this 1909
corn crop. "The priceless gift of
the Indian" as Secretary Wilson
calls corn, is waving it's tassels
over the largest part Of the middle
west-an area that would make a
belt of emerald stalks seven miles
wide around the equator; a terri
tory three quarters as large as
France or Germany, and so per
cent greater than England, Ire
land and Scotland combined; an
expanse large enough to allow a
double track railway from earth to
Mars!


ITS mighty hot" one farmer
remarks to another hereabouts
when they meet.


That's SO, but it's mighty fine
corn weather." is the comforting
reply.


Greatest of all cereal crops
corn comes last in the category OF
harvests. The billion two hun
dred millions of dollars that the
farmers will get for their corr
this year, in one form or another
shows the remarkable power Of
the farmer in economy. When he
raises wheat he raises 1t entirely
to sell. But when the farmer har
vests corn his methods immedi
/ ately become those of the business
man. He will market only 20;
/ per cent this year in cereal form.
4 The other 1917 per cent he man
ufactures into beef and pork. Or


the marvellous yield depends the
huge traffic Of the packing houses
and the solution Of the worlds


food problem.


i Lean range cattle are snatchet


up by the corn raiser, who fattenl
them with his own corn during thu


winter. Then they are sent in prime


market condition to the packing


house centers, and the farmer no


only gets his profit from his corn


but an added profit for the beef h,


sells and still another profit on tn
